goal: Deliver a Go Convex client with behavioral 1:1 parity to convex-rs.

steps.name: Set up step-level validation harness
steps.description: Create a small runner that maps each step id to its required validation commands and artifacts. Guidance: this harness is used after every implementation step, not only at the end.
success_factors: Runner outputs pass/fail by step id
Validation evidence is saved in a predictable location

steps.name: Add per-step validation manifest
steps.description: Introduce a machine-readable manifest linking step serials to required test targets, fixture checks, and manual verifications. Guidance: keep the manifest versioned and reviewed with each change.
success_factors: All implementation steps have mapped validations

steps.name: Create remediation workflow template
steps.description: Define a standard remediation flow for any failed validation: reproduce, isolate, patch, regression-test, and document. Guidance: remediation outputs should reference failing step ids.
success_factors: Every failure can be traced to root cause and fix

steps.name: Add auto-generated remediation backlog
steps.description: When a validation fails, automatically create a remediation backlog entry with owner, scope, failing test, and exit criteria. Guidance: no failure is allowed to remain undocumented.
success_factors: Backlog items include clear exit criteria
Open remediations are visible in status reviews

steps.name: Add step-level failure triage checklist
steps.description: Create a triage checklist used whenever any step validation fails. Guidance: classify failures as spec gap, implementation bug, test bug, or fixture drift, then assign concrete follow-up.
success_factors: Each failed step gets triage classification

steps.name: Add remediation regression test requirement
steps.description: Require every remediation to include a regression test before closure. Guidance: no remediation closes on code change alone.
success_factors: Regression tests fail before fix and pass after

steps.name: Add pre-merge validation policy
steps.description: Define mandatory validations required before merging any parity-impacting change. Guidance: tie policy to step ids and parity checklist ids.
success_factors: Merges are blocked on unmet validations

steps.name: Run phase gate dry run with seeded failures
steps.description: Intentionally seed known failures and confirm the validation plus remediation workflow catches and routes them correctly. Guidance: this validates the process itself before relying on it.

steps.name: Finalize continuous validation readiness
steps.description: Confirm step-level validation, phase gates, and remediation loops are operational before declaring implementation execution ready. Guidance: this is the readiness check for the whole delivery process.
success_factors: Team can execute steps with confidence

steps.name: Pin Rust baseline
steps.description: Record the exact convex-rs commit hash and tag used as the parity reference. Guidance: save the hash in repo docs so all implementation and tests target one immutable source.
success_factors: No work proceeds against an unpinned baseline

steps.name: Build API parity matrix
steps.description: List every public Rust API symbol and map it to intended Go API shape. Guidance: include methods, types, constructors, callbacks, and stream contracts.
success_factors: All public Rust APIs are mapped
Go equivalent exists or a justified note is added

steps.name: Build protocol parity matrix
steps.description: Map all client/server wire message variants and fields from sync_types to Go representations. Guidance: include optional fields and defaulting behavior.
success_factors: Field naming and wire keys are captured

steps.name: Define validation policy
steps.description: Create a repo-level validation policy describing required checks per subsystem. Guidance: no step may be marked complete without an attached validation method.

steps.name: Add parity checklist artifact
steps.description: Create a machine-readable checklist that maps every parity item to at least one test. Guidance: keep item ids stable for CI reporting.
success_factors: Every parity item has an id
Each id maps to test coverage

steps.name: Create Go module skeleton
steps.description: Create package layout for public API plus internal modules. Guidance: keep boundaries aligned to Rust domains (client, base state machine, protocol, sync transport, value).

steps.name: Set up CI and quality gates
steps.description: Add CI tasks for unit tests, race detector, lint, and format checks. Guidance: keep gates mandatory before step completion in later phases.
success_factors: CI runs go test and go test -race

steps.name: Add deterministic test utilities
steps.description: Introduce clock/rng abstractions for repeatable backoff and timeout tests. Guidance: avoid sleeping-based flaky tests where deterministic hooks are possible.

steps.name: Add API signature conformance tests
steps.description: Implement compile-time or reflection-based checks to ensure intended public API signatures remain intact. Guidance: fail fast on accidental API drift.

steps.name: Add public type shape conformance tests
steps.description: Validate key exported types and enums/variants maintain expected structure. Guidance: include FunctionResult, Value, auth token types, and websocket state.
success_factors: Type-shape tests cover all key exports

steps.name: Implement Value type
steps.description: Create Go representation for Convex values: null, int64, float64, bool, string, bytes, array, object. Guidance: choose a shape that preserves type identity without ambiguity.
success_factors: All Rust Value variants are represented

steps.name: Implement ConvexError and FunctionResult
steps.description: Add FunctionResult variants equivalent to Rust and conversion rules from error payloads. Guidance: preserve distinction between plain message errors and ConvexError data payloads.
success_factors: Error payload conversion logic matches Rust

steps.name: Encode Value to JSON wire format
steps.description: Implement value encoding rules including `$integer`, `$float`, `$bytes`. Guidance: preserve Rust behavior for NaN, infinities, and negative zero.

steps.name: Decode JSON wire format to Value
steps.description: Implement Value decode with strict validation and unsupported-type error behavior. Guidance: reject deprecated `$set` and `$map` the same way Rust does.
success_factors: Decode paths handle all supported tags

steps.name: Add Value roundtrip tests
steps.description: Create unit and fuzz/property-style tests for Value encode/decode. Guidance: include trophy cases for edge float and nested payload structures.

steps.name: Implement core protocol identifiers
steps.description: Add Go types for QueryId, SessionId, IdentityVersion, QuerySetVersion, StateVersion, Timestamp, and request sequence numbers. Guidance: keep conversion boundaries explicit.

steps.name: Implement auth and identity structs
steps.description: Create AuthenticationToken variants and user identity attributes. Guidance: include admin acting-as payload and token none behavior.

steps.name: Implement query and state modification types
steps.description: Port Query, QuerySetModification, StateModification, and related payload types. Guidance: mirror wire-facing field names and optional journals.
success_factors: Type parity table marks this area complete

steps.name: Implement ClientMessage model
steps.description: Add all ClientMessage variants including Connect, ModifyQuerySet, Mutation, Action, Authenticate, and Event. Guidance: keep branch behavior explicit for serialization.

steps.name: Implement ServerMessage model
steps.description: Add all ServerMessage variants including Transition, MutationResponse, ActionResponse, AuthError, FatalError, TransitionChunk, and Ping. Guidance: model success/error payload unions carefully.

steps.name: Implement ClientMessage codec
steps.description: Write JSON marshal/unmarshal for client messages with Rust-compatible tags and field defaults. Guidance: reproduce base64 timestamp handling exactly where required.

steps.name: Implement ServerMessage codec
steps.description: Write JSON marshal/unmarshal for server messages including success/error branches and optional fields. Guidance: keep error-data payload behavior identical to Rust.
success_factors: Success and error branches are correct

steps.name: Add golden protocol fixtures
steps.description: Create fixture tests to lock exact JSON shape for representative messages. Guidance: include every variant and at least one edge-case payload per variant family.
success_factors: Fixture diffs are easy to review

steps.name: Add protocol fixture generator from Rust
steps.description: Generate protocol fixture vectors from the Rust baseline to use as Go conformance data. Guidance: pin fixture generation to the baseline commit hash.

steps.name: Validate client message fixtures
steps.description: Run Go encode/decode checks against Rust-generated client message fixtures. Guidance: validate both directions and exact field presence.
success_factors: Encode output matches expected wire form

steps.name: Validate server message fixtures
steps.description: Run Go encode/decode checks against Rust-generated server message fixtures. Guidance: include success and error payload variants.

steps.name: Validate malformed protocol payloads
steps.description: Create negative tests for invalid/missing protocol fields and type mismatches. Guidance: errors should be deterministic and useful.

steps.name: Add fuzz tests for message codecs
steps.description: Fuzz client/server message JSON decode and encode paths. Guidance: focus on panics, hangs, and silently accepted invalid states.
success_factors: Fuzz target compiles and runs in CI or nightly

steps.name: Add fuzz tests for value codec
steps.description: Fuzz Value encode/decode including nested and edge numeric payloads. Guidance: include mixed object/array depth and invalid tag objects.

steps.name: Validate u64 base64 timestamp conversions
steps.description: Add exact-vector tests for timestamp base64 conversions used on the wire. Guidance: include min, max, and random boundary values.

steps.name: Validate special float encode/decode matrix
steps.description: Validate NaN, +/-Inf, and -0 encoding rules against Rust behavior. Guidance: assert when `$float` must be used versus plain JSON number.

steps.name: Validate bytes encoding matrix
steps.description: Test byte-array wire encoding/decoding across sizes and values. Guidance: include empty, binary-heavy, and large payload cases.

steps.name: Validate integer boundary matrix
steps.description: Validate int64 boundary encoding and decoding at key limits. Guidance: include min/max int64 and near-boundary values.

steps.name: Validate nested object determinism
steps.description: Assert deterministic object serialization behavior for parity-sensitive tokenization paths. Guidance: verify map ordering does not alter canonical semantics.

steps.name: Validate unsupported $set/$map error parity
steps.description: Assert decode errors for deprecated `$set` and `$map` match intended behavior. Guidance: keep messages stable enough for debugging.
success_factors: Parity with Rust behavior is verified

steps.name: Validation gate after protocol/value implementation
steps.description: Run full protocol/value conformance checks immediately after steps 20-44 and before continuing. Guidance: block progress until conformance and negative tests pass.
success_factors: Any remediations are closed before proceeding

steps.name: Continuous parity diff checks against Rust
steps.description: Schedule periodic parity diff checks that compare current Go behavior against Rust baseline fixtures as implementation evolves. Guidance: run these checks at each phase gate.
success_factors: Diff results are reviewed at each gate

steps.name: Implement backoff with jitter
steps.description: Port backoff behavior including reset and failure-count progression. Guidance: match Rust timing formula and max cap semantics.

steps.name: Implement RequestManager
steps.description: Create request tracking for mutation/action with completion state, timestamps, and replay ordering. Guidance: keep pending/completed transitions explicit.
success_factors: Type mismatch and invalid id paths are handled

steps.name: Test RequestManager semantics
steps.description: Add targeted tests for immediate action completion, errored mutation completion, and timestamp-gated mutation completion. Guidance: replicate Rust behavior test-by-test.

steps.name: Create LocalSyncState skeleton
steps.description: Build internal state for query set, query ids, versions, latest results, and auth fetcher. Guidance: keep mutating operations confined to the base client worker owner.

steps.name: Implement query token canonicalization
steps.description: Port canonical `(udfPath,args)` token serialization used for dedupe. Guidance: maintain deterministic ordering for object keys and canonicalized path handling.
success_factors: Equivalent queries map to same token
Distinct args map to distinct tokens

steps.name: Implement subscribe path
steps.description: Port subscribe logic including dedupe, subscriber index incrementing, and outgoing ModifyQuerySet generation. Guidance: preserve subscriber-id uniqueness rules.
success_factors: Subscriber ids are unique and stable

steps.name: Implement unsubscribe path
steps.description: Port unsubscribe behavior including remove message only when final subscriber is dropped. Guidance: ensure dropped unknown subscriber is treated as internal invariant failure.
success_factors: Intermediate unsubscribes do not remove query

steps.name: Implement auth fetcher semantics
steps.description: Add set/clear auth fetcher behavior with immediate fetch (`force_refresh=false`) and stored callback for reconnect (`force_refresh=true`). Guidance: match base version increment behavior.
success_factors: Initial auth send behavior is correct
Callback storage and refresh are correct
Clear auth path emits None token

steps.name: Implement transition ingest pipeline
steps.description: Port remote query set transition handling, version checks, and query result ingestion. Guidance: preserve start_version mismatch error behavior.
success_factors: Version mismatch errors trigger reconnect path
State modifications update local results correctly

steps.name: Implement response handling for non-transition messages
steps.description: Port mutation response, action response, auth error, fatal error, ping, and transition chunk behavior. Guidance: transition chunk must be treated as protocol error as in Rust.
success_factors: Transition chunk path returns expected error

steps.name: Implement reconnect replay path
steps.description: Port resend logic that clears stale queue, regenerates auth/query messages, and appends ongoing mutations. Guidance: preserve stale-version bug fix behavior.
success_factors: Auth and query set are rebuilt correctly
Regression test for duplicate base_version passes

steps.name: Implement QueryResults view
steps.description: Add result-view structure mapping subscriber ids to query results with iterator-like access. Guidance: preserve behavior where subscriber may exist before first value arrives.
success_factors: Iteration exposes subscribers with optional values

steps.name: Port BaseConvexClient test suite
steps.description: Write Go tests covering base state machine behaviors from Rust tests, including reconnect auth refresh and stale queue prevention. Guidance: use deterministic fake protocol messages.
success_factors: Key Rust behavior tests are mirrored
Tests pass reliably under race detector

steps.name: Validate query token determinism across map ordering
steps.description: Explicitly test query token serialization when args map insertion order varies. Guidance: token equality must depend on content, not insertion history.
success_factors: Token equality holds for equivalent args
Token inequality holds for distinct args

steps.name: Validate query dedupe behavior
steps.description: Test that duplicate subscribe calls dedupe query registration while maintaining independent subscribers. Guidance: ensure only required ModifyQuerySet messages are emitted.
success_factors: No redundant add messages are sent

steps.name: Validate subscriber id uniqueness under churn
steps.description: Stress subscribe/unsubscribe cycles to ensure subscriber id uniqueness and no collisions. Guidance: include rapid churn and same-query cycles.

steps.name: Validate unsubscribe decrement semantics
steps.description: Confirm intermediate unsubscribe operations decrement subscriber count without removing query. Guidance: inspect queue emissions and current results.
success_factors: Query remains active for remaining subscribers

steps.name: Validate final unsubscribe remove emission
steps.description: Confirm final subscriber drop emits exactly one remove modification. Guidance: check version transitions and no duplicate removes.

steps.name: Validate query set version monotonicity
steps.description: Add invariant tests ensuring query set base/new versions move monotonically and consistently. Guidance: include add/remove/reconnect sequences.

steps.name: Validate identity version monotonicity
steps.description: Verify identity version updates for auth set/clear/refresh paths. Guidance: include callback and static token flows.

steps.name: Validate auth callback immediate fetch behavior
steps.description: Ensure setting auth callback triggers immediate fetch with non-refresh flag. Guidance: assert invocation count and arguments.

steps.name: Validate auth callback refresh-on-reconnect behavior
steps.description: Ensure reconnect path invokes auth callback with refresh semantics and uses returned token. Guidance: verify replay message ordering too.
success_factors: Force-refresh flag is true on reconnect

steps.name: Validate auth-clear behavior
steps.description: Validate clearing auth sends None token and resets state correctly. Guidance: include set->clear->set transitions.

steps.name: Validate mutation response completion gate by transition
steps.description: Assert successful mutation completion waits for transition visibility semantics. Guidance: mimic Rust test timing behavior.

steps.name: Validate mutation immediate completion on error
steps.description: Assert errored mutations complete immediately without waiting for transition. Guidance: include error payload forms.

steps.name: Validate action immediate completion
steps.description: Assert actions complete upon response and do not require transition gating. Guidance: cover success and failure action payloads.
success_factors: Success and error actions are handled

steps.name: Validate request replay ordering on reconnect
steps.description: Ensure replay ordering for ongoing requests matches expected semantics after reconnect. Guidance: assert deterministic ordering in tests.

steps.name: Validate outgoing queue clear before replay
steps.description: Add regression tests for stale queue clearing before reconnect replay messages are enqueued. Guidance: explicitly reproduce prior stale-version failure mode.

steps.name: Validate max observed timestamp tracking
steps.description: Verify observed timestamp monotonic updates from transitions and mutation responses. Guidance: include mixed arrival order cases.

steps.name: Validate transition start version mismatch failure
steps.description: Ensure transition start version mismatch produces protocol failure and reconnect path trigger. Guidance: assert specific failure classification.

steps.name: Validate TransitionChunk rejection behavior
steps.description: Ensure TransitionChunk is rejected per Rust behavior for this client. Guidance: verify error propagation and recovery path.

steps.name: Validate Ping no-op behavior
steps.description: Confirm Ping messages do not mutate state or complete pending operations. Guidance: include high-frequency ping scenarios.

steps.name: Validation gate after base state machine implementation
steps.description: Run targeted base-client semantic tests immediately after steps 47-78. Guidance: include reconnect replay, versioning, mutation completion, and auth refresh behavior.

steps.name: Define SyncProtocol interface
steps.description: Create protocol interface and shared response/request types used by worker and websocket manager. Guidance: include reconnect request reason and max observed timestamp fields.

steps.name: Implement deployment URL to WS URL conversion
steps.description: Port URL conversion logic from deployment HTTP(S) to websocket `/api/sync` endpoint. Guidance: preserve invalid scheme error behavior.
success_factors: Unsupported schemes fail with clear error

steps.name: Implement websocket connect handshake
steps.description: Build ws manager open path with Convex-Client header and initial Connect message send. Guidance: include session id generation and reconnect metadata fields.
success_factors: Connect message emitted with required fields

steps.name: Implement websocket read and send loops
steps.description: Handle incoming text messages, decode to ServerMessage, and forward protocol responses while supporting outbound sends with acknowledgements. Guidance: keep send path non-blocking and error-aware.
success_factors: Incoming messages are parsed and forwarded

steps.name: Implement heartbeat and inactivity logic
steps.description: Port heartbeat interval and server inactivity timeout handling. Guidance: on inactivity, trigger failure path that coordinates reconnect with the worker.

steps.name: Validate websocket state callback transitions
steps.description: Validate emitted websocket states across connect, reconnect, and failure cycles. Guidance: assert ordering and repeat behavior.

steps.name: Validate websocket inactivity timeout path
steps.description: Test inactivity threshold detection and transition into reconnect workflow. Guidance: use deterministic clock to avoid flakes.

steps.name: Validate websocket backoff growth and cap
steps.description: Verify exponential jittered backoff behavior and max cap under repeated failures. Guidance: use deterministic RNG for predictable assertions.

steps.name: Validate websocket backoff reset after success
steps.description: Ensure backoff resets after healthy communication, matching Rust reset points. Guidance: assert failure count transitions.

steps.name: Validate websocket send failure propagation
steps.description: Test send path failures and verify error propagation through worker and reconnect logic. Guidance: include closed socket and transient send errors.

steps.name: Validation gate after transport implementation
steps.description: Run transport-specific validation immediately after steps 80-89. Guidance: include heartbeat, inactivity timeout, send failures, and backoff progression checks.
success_factors: Timeout and failure paths are stable

steps.name: Implement worker main loop
steps.description: Create worker orchestrating base client, protocol manager, and request channels. Guidance: match Rust order of operations, including pre-select message flush.
success_factors: Worker loop processes protocol and client requests

steps.name: Validate worker flush-before-select ordering
steps.description: Verify worker flushes queued outbound messages before normal select loop processing. Guidance: test with reconnect-generated queued work.

steps.name: Validate protocol failure triggers reconnect request
steps.description: Ensure protocol failure responses trigger reconnect requests with reason and max observed timestamp. Guidance: assert exact reconnect payload fields.

steps.name: Validate communicate loop handles responses during send
steps.description: Assert worker continues processing protocol responses while waiting for send completion. Guidance: protect against deadlock regression.
success_factors: Responses are processed during send wait

steps.name: Validation gate after worker orchestration
steps.description: Run orchestration and ordering validations immediately after steps 91-94. Guidance: verify flush-before-select, response processing during send, and reconnect request propagation.
success_factors: Issues found are remediated and re-tested

steps.name: Implement high-level ConvexClient and builder
steps.description: Expose high-level API constructors and configuration options (client id, state callback). Guidance: maintain one shared connection model across clones.
success_factors: Client starts worker and protocol manager
Clone/share behavior is explicit and tested

steps.name: Implement subscriptions and watch_all
steps.description: Port single-query and query-set subscription abstractions with unsubscribe-on-drop/close behavior. Guidance: include initial value delivery semantics and lag handling strategy.

steps.name: Implement query, mutation, action APIs
steps.description: Add high-level operation methods and map them to worker requests. Guidance: query must be sugar over subscribe-first-value behavior.

steps.name: Implement auth APIs
steps.description: Add set_auth, set_auth_callback, and admin auth helper behavior on top of worker auth requests. Guidance: static-token path should wrap callback semantics like Rust.
success_factors: Static token and callback paths both work

steps.name: Implement client lifecycle semantics
steps.description: Port drop/close behavior so final client reference tears down background resources safely. Guidance: avoid goroutine leaks and closed-channel panics.
success_factors: Final close shuts down worker and transport

steps.name: Validate query sugar equals subscribe-first-value
steps.description: Test `Query` behavior equivalence to subscribe plus first stream item semantics. Guidance: verify both result and timing behavior.

steps.name: Validate watch_all consistency snapshots
steps.description: Assert watch_all emits consistent cross-query snapshots with correct subscriber/result mapping. Guidance: include partial update and missing-value states.
success_factors: Partial states are represented as expected

steps.name: Validate clone shares connection and subscriptions
steps.description: Ensure client clone semantics share underlying connection and active subscription state. Guidance: verify operations from multiple clones interoperate correctly.
success_factors: No duplicate transport instances are created

steps.name: Validate final client close semantics and stream termination
steps.description: Verify last client close tears down background routines and closes subscription streams predictably. Guidance: include goroutine leak checks.

steps.name: Validation gate after high-level API implementation
steps.description: Run high-level API behavior checks immediately after steps 96-104. Guidance: include clone semantics, query sugar behavior, and subscription lifecycle behavior.

steps.name: Build test protocol manager
steps.description: Create fake protocol manager for deterministic unit tests, equivalent to Rust testing sync manager. Guidance: include sent-message capture and fake server response injection.
success_factors: Worker tests use this harness successfully

steps.name: Port high-level client behavior tests
steps.description: Translate Rust client behavior tests to Go covering subscribe patterns, query sugar, mutation/action semantics, auth, and URL conversion. Guidance: keep one-to-one intent with Rust tests.
success_factors: Core Rust behavior tests are mirrored
Race detector passes for client tests

steps.name: Validation gate after test harness implementation
steps.description: After steps 106-107, run meta-validation to ensure tests themselves match intended Rust behavior and catch expected regressions. Guidance: validate test quality, not only implementation code.

steps.name: Add integration tests against live Convex
steps.description: Add opt-in integration tests that run query/mutation/action/subscribe against a real deployment. Guidance: gate with env vars and isolate test data names.

steps.name: Write examples and API docs
steps.description: Provide quickstart and chat-style examples equivalent to Rust examples. Guidance: include builder/auth usage and subscription loop examples.
success_factors: Docs include realtime and auth patterns

steps.name: Finalize parity audit and release gate
steps.description: Run final parity audit against matrices and close gaps before release candidate. Guidance: unresolved differences require explicit sign-off with rationale.
success_factors: All tests and CI gates are green

steps.name: Final validation gate: race, integration, parity signoff
steps.description: Run full validation suite including race tests, integration tests, and parity checklist signoff. Guidance: block release unless every checklist id is verified.
success_factors: Integration suite passes in configured environment

steps.name: Add weekly conformance sweep
steps.description: Run a full conformance sweep at regular cadence during development, not just near release. Guidance: include protocol fixtures, semantic tests, race tests, and integration smoke tests.

steps.name: Add exception handling policy for unavoidable drift
steps.description: Define a strict process for documenting, approving, and tracking any temporary drift from Rust behavior. Guidance: each exception must include owner, rationale, and retirement date.
success_factors: Retirement plan exists for each exception

ordering_policy: The steps array order is the execution order and serial values are sequential IDs.
